channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Says New Air Force One Will Be Taken Out of Service to Be ‘Maxed Out’
item.description: The Qatari-donated plane has been under scrutiny over whether it is fitted with sufficient defensive capabilities to adequately protect a U.S. president and others traveling on it.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman
item.media:description: President Trump exiting the Qatari-donated jet earlier this month in South Dakota.

item.title: JD and Usha Vance Welcome Fourth Child
item.description: In a post on social media, Mr. Vance said his wife and their son, Alec Neel Vance, were “happy and healthy.”
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance and Usha Vance during an April event at the White House.

item.title: Trump Relents From Spain-Bashing to Present Its Team the World Cup Trophy
item.description: Mr. Trump, who this month referred to Spain as “hopeless,” seemingly had little choice but to exhibit diplomacy on Sunday as he handed the golden trophy to the country’s players.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor and Christopher Maag
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump presents Lamine Yamal with a medal after Spain’s victory over Argentina at the 2026 World Cup final in New Jersey on Sunday.

item.title: Federal Agents Were Told FBI Will No Longer Investigate ICE Confrontations
item.description: Senior officials deny such a change is in the works, despite guidance shared with agents around the country in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: Crime scene tape near the vehicle of Renee Good, who was shot and killed by ICE agents in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: In Maine Senate Race, Jackson Sprints Ahead of Pack, as Bellows Drops Out
item.description: Troy Jackson, a progressive, solidified his clear edge in the contest to replace Graham Platner, as Shenna Bellows, Jordan Wood and Nirav Shah ended their bids.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner, Sydney Cromwell and Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
item.media:description: Troy Jackson at a candidate debate last week in Portland, Maine.

item.title: Politicians Are Trying to Change What Chatbots Say About Them
item.description: Political campaigns have something new to worry about these days: unflattering or incomplete information produced by A.I. programs when voters ask questions about candidates.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson and Tiffany Hsu
item.media:credit: Clayton Steward for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dustin Lloyd, a Democratic primary candidate for Missouri’s state legislature, has learned how to influence what A.I. tells voters about his campaign.

item.title: Trump Blasts ‘Sanctuary’ States, but Many Still Aid Deportations
item.description: A New York Times review of 18 states targeted by the Trump administration for their “sanctuary” policies found that all cooperate significantly with immigration authorities in cases involving violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jazmine Ulloa, Miriam Jordan and Albert Sun
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Federal agents and local police outside an immigration processing center in Broadview, Ill., last fall.

item.title: Trump Gutted Civil Rights Agency, Stifling U.S. Scrutiny of Workplace Bias
item.description: A little-known federal office spent decades investigating potential discrimination by government contractors — until it was stopped by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Coral Davenport
item.category: Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs
item.media:credit: Kristen Zeis for The New York Times
item.media:description: BAE Systems, a company that makes weapons, vehicles and cybersecurity tools for the Pentagon, was investigated for two years by a Labor Department civil rights office until President Trump signed an executive order that shut down the inquiry.

item.title: In Maine, Troy Jackson Gains Momentum in Bid to Replace Platner
item.description: Troy Jackson, a progressive former State Senate president, showed signs of significant strength, as half the state’s counties picked delegates to a convention where they will choose Graham Platner’s replacement.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner, Sydney Cromwell and Tim Balk
item.media:credit: John Tully for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters met to select delegates who will represent their county to choose the nominee for U.S. Senate at the Maine Democratic Party’s Nominating Convention.

item.title: Cindy Burbank, Nebraska’s Democratic Senate Nominee, Files to Withdraw From Race
item.description: The move by Cindy Burbank could clear a path for Dan Osborn, an independent candidate backed by her party’s leadership. But Republicans have cried foul, and her effort could face obstacles.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Walker Pickering for The New York Times

item.title: A Very British CPAC: Few, if Any, Costumes and Not Much Trump
item.description: London hosted an understated version of the Conservative Political Action Conference, known in the United States for its over-the-top celebration of MAGA causes.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Matt Schlapp, chairman of the Conservative Political Action Conference, and the former prime minister Liz Truss onstage at CPAC’s London conference on Thursday.

item.title: These AIPAC-Backed Democrats Voted to Nix Israel Aid. AIPAC Punished Them.
item.description: Of the 103 Democrats who voted to eliminate aid to Israel, more than a dozen had been endorsed by the pro-Israel lobbying group. AIPAC quickly closed off online donations for them.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

item.title: The Evolution of Presidential Travel: Horse Carriages to Air Force One
item.description: From horse-drawn presidential coaches to Air Force One, presidents have used various modes of transportation to project an image of America for nearly 250 years.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

item.title: Zohran Mamdani Knows He Has Political Capital. And He Intends to Spend It.
item.description: Lulu Garcia-Navarro sits down for an interview with the mayor of New York City.
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
item.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: Without Platner, Maine Democrats Scramble to Keep Grassroots Energy Alive
item.description: Graham Platner’s campaign for Senate imploded last week. The activists who backed him are seeking a candidate to carry his populist message in the race against Republican Susan Collins.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes
item.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Our movement was not solely about Graham, even though he brought us together,” said Ryan Warren, a former volunteer for Graham Platner’s Senate campaign.

item.title: Russia Bill Broadens Sanctions but Could Fuel Fears Over the Dollar
item.description: The Trump administration has recently sought to scale back sanctions as adversaries develop workarounds that some believe could erode the U.S. dollar’s supremacy.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of Foreign Assets Control (United States Treasury)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: As Trump Scraps With Meloni, His Envoy to Italy Is at Sea
item.description: Tilman J. Fertitta, the U.S. ambassador to Rome, is summering on his superyacht as President Trump squabbles with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Gianni Cipriano
item.media:description: Tilman Fertitta aboard his yacht, Boardwalk, on the Fourth of July.

item.title: AIPAC Closes Donor Portal to Democrats Who Voted to End Aid to Israel
item.description: The pro-Israel lobbying group appeared to be punishing Democrats it had endorsed for re-election after they voted in favor of an amendment that would have cut off U.S. aid to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: In AIPAC’s web portal, the option of donating to House Democrats who voted to end U.S. aid to Israel was unavailable. That included Representative Katherine Clark of Massachusetts, left.

item.title: Trump Endorses Graham for Senate, Scrambling South Carolina Primary
item.description: Senator Darline Graham had been seen as a caretaker for her brother’s seat after his sudden death. Now she has the president’s nod in what was shaping up to be a crowded fight to succeed Lindsey Graham.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Darline Graham, left, was sworn in on Tuesday at the Capitol.

item.title: Who Really Has the 2026 Midterms Cash Edge?
item.description: The fund-raising race is tighter than it seems.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood
item.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of Ken Paxton last month at the 2026 Republican Party of Texas convention in Houston. Mr. Paxton, the party’s nominee for Senate, has been drastically out-raised by his Democratic rival, James Talarico.
